sources.notes: 题面与约束：无向图，边权可在 [l_i,r_i] 内选择；n,m 总和 <= 2e5，判断是否能让 dist(1,n) != dist(1,k)+dist(k,n)。
官方题解给出 robber/cops 解释：先用 r 权从 k 跑最短路，再用 l 权跑带危险阈值的改造 Dijkstra。
洛谷题解交叉确认极值化：存在解时可令某条目标路径边取 L，其余边取 R，并把条件化为路径前缀 DP 后跑 Dijkstra。
Maspy 题解独立证明 L/R 极值化与 P_{vw}<d_v+d_w 的充要条件，并给出 Dijkstra 式 DP。

reference_candidates.notes: Luogu 候选汇总页；本草稿实际参考的是具体专栏文章。

annotation.summary: 给每条边在 [l,r] 中选权，判断是否能让 1 到 n 的最短距离严格小于经 k 的距离之和，即不存在等长最短路必须经 k。
annotation.core_idea: 由于三角不等式总有 dist(1,n) <= dist(1,k)+dist(k,n)，目标是制造严格小于。若存在解，可固定一条 1->n 候选路径 P，使 P 上边取 L，其余边取 R。先在全 R 权图上从 k 跑 Dijkstra 得 d[v]。路径 P 合法等价于对 P 上任意 u,v，P 段长 < d[u]+d[v]。把该全局条件压成到达每个点时的单个危险值 x：经过 L 边 u->v 后 x' = max(x + L_e, -d[v])，若 x >= d[u] 则当前位置已不安全。按最小 x 的 Dijkstra 方式扩展，若能安全到 n 则 YES。
annotation.complexity: O((n+m) log n) per test case
tricks: 把连续边权选择压到路径边取 L、非路径边取 R 的极值结构
用 k 到各点的 R 权最短路作为危险阈值
把任意两点路径不等式压缩成可 Dijkstra 维护的一维状态

solution_variants.name: official_robber_dijkstra
solution_variants.summary: 先用 R 权从 k 跑普通 Dijkstra 得警察到达阈值 d[v]；再从 1 出发用 L 权和状态 x 跑改造 Dijkstra，状态转移为 max(x+L_e,-d[v])，只扩展 x<d[u] 的点。

solution_variants.name: prefix_dp_dijkstra
solution_variants.summary: 把路径合法性写成对所有前缀点的 P_{uv}<d[u]+d[v]，维护到当前点的最坏 P_{uv}-d[u] 值，转移同样可用 Dijkstra 式贪心确定。

tags.description: Shortest path problems whose main algorithm is Dijkstra's algorithm or a Dijkstra-style priority-queue relaxation over nonnegative edge transitions; use for ordinary and modified Dijkstra when the priority key is the decisive state.
tags.created_reason: Existing graph/shortest-path is too broad for querying problems specifically solved by Dijkstra or Dijkstra-like greedy relaxation.
tags.evidence: The solution performs an ordinary Dijkstra from k under R weights, then a second Dijkstra-style priority queue relaxation over the danger state.
A crucial proof reduces arbitrary chosen edge weights to an extreme assignment: one candidate 1->n path uses L, all other edges use R.
The path-wide strict triangle condition is compressed into an augmented scalar state x carried by Dijkstra.
The modified transition uses x' = max(x+L_e,-d[v]), so the path key is a minimax-style value rather than a plain sum.
Correctness of the second phase relies on settling the currently best danger value first, analogous to Dijkstra's greedy invariant.
The graph condition is transformed through the strict inequality P_{uv}<d[u]+d[v] for all pairs of points on the chosen path.
